audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's reply to Bildad: sarcastic critique of unhelpful counsel, cosmic sovereignty, Sheol/Abaddon exposure, earth suspended over nothing, sea/dragon imagery, and whisper of divine power.

verses.restored: How you have helped one without power! How you have saved an arm without strength!
How you have counseled one without wisdom and made sound wisdom known in abundance!
With whose help have you uttered words, and whose breath has gone out from you?
The shades tremble beneath the waters and their inhabitants.
Sheol is naked before him, and Abaddon has no covering.
He stretches north over emptiness and hangs the earth over nothing.
He binds waters in his clouds, and the cloud is not torn beneath them.
He covers the face of the full moon, spreading his cloud over it.
He has inscribed a circle on the face of the waters, at the boundary between light and darkness.
The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his rebuke.
By his power he stilled the sea; by his understanding he shattered Rahab.
By his breath the heavens became fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
Look, these are the edges of his ways, and how small a whisper we hear of him! But the thunder of his power, who can understand?
